title: How to Keep Linux Updated and Secure
description: Learn how to update your Linux system, enable automatic security updates, and set up a basic firewall.
prerequisites: A Linux distribution installed (Ubuntu, Debian, Mint, etc.)

steps.title: Update from the terminal
steps.content: The most reliable way to update your system. `apt update` refreshes the list of available packages, and `apt upgrade` installs the new versions.
steps.code: sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade

steps.title: Update from the GUI
steps.content:
Don't want to use the terminal? Most distros have a graphical updater:

- **Ubuntu**: Software Updater (opens automatically when updates are available)
- **Linux Mint**: Update Manager (lives in the system tray)
- **Pop!_OS**: Settings → OS Upgrade & Recovery

Click the notification or open the updater from the app menu.

steps.title: Enable automatic security updates
steps.content: Let your system install critical security patches automatically in the background. This only applies to security updates — not app upgrades.
steps.code:
sudo apt install unattended-upgrades
sudo dpkg-reconfigure --priority=low unattended-upgrades

steps.title: Clean up old packages
steps.content: Over time, unused packages and old kernels pile up. Clean them periodically to free space.
steps.code:
sudo apt autoremove
sudo apt autoclean

steps.title: Set up a basic firewall
steps.content: UFW (Uncomplicated Firewall) is a simple firewall that comes with Ubuntu. Enable it to block unwanted incoming connections while allowing your normal internet use.
steps.code:
sudo ufw enable
sudo ufw status

# Allow SSH if you need remote access:
sudo ufw allow ssh

steps.title: Update Flatpak and Snap apps
steps.content: Flatpak and Snap apps update separately from your system packages. Snaps update automatically, but you can trigger it manually. Flatpak needs a manual command.
steps.code:
# Update all Flatpak apps:
flatpak update

# Update all Snap apps:
sudo snap refresh

seo.title: How to Keep Linux Updated and Secure — Beginner Guide
seo.description: Learn how to update your Linux system, enable automatic security updates, clean up old packages, and set up a basic firewall with UFW.
